plan:
Step 1: Update quality tier config in `src/visual/quality.ts`
- Add `terrainPointCount` field to `QualityProfile` (low: 20000, medium: 60000, high: 120000)
- Keep `terrainRows`/`terrainCols` for heightfield sampling grid resolution (internal to generator, not point count)
- Add `terrainPointCount` to `COUNT_FIELDS` and `COUNT_MINIMUMS` (min: 5000)
- Update `extractSystemConfig('terrain', ...)` to return `{ rows, cols, noiseOctaves, pointCount }`

Step 2: Create new generator `src/visual/generators/terrainParticleSheet.ts`
- New function `generateTerrainParticleSheet(opts: { rows, cols, pointCount, seed, width?, depth?, heightScale?, octaves? })`
- Samples `pointCount` points across the heightfield extent using seeded PRNG
- Each point gets a random (x, z) within the grid bounds (NOT snapped to grid intersections)
- Adds small per-cell jitter so no visible row/column alignment
- Computes initial FBM height for each point (same FBM as existing generator)
- Returns `{ positions: Float32Array (pointCount*3), randoms: Float32Array (pointCount*3), pointCount }`
- Reuse existing `createPRNG` and `hashRandomFromPosition` utilities

Step 3: Rewrite system `src/visual/systems/terrainHeightfield.ts`
- Remove `edgeMesh` (LineSegments) entirely — no more wireframe grid edges
- Remove imports of edge shaders (`terrain.vert.glsl`, `terrain.frag.glsl`)
- Remove `extractUniqueVertices` import (no longer deduplicating edge endpoints)
- Import new `generateTerrainParticleSheet` instead of `generateTerrainHeightfield`
- Accept `pointCount` in `TerrainHeightfieldConfig`
- Single `THREE.Points` mesh with dense particle geometry from new generator
- Keep existing additive blending, transparent, depthWrite:false material settings
- Keep spatial gradient vertex colors (compute via `computeVertexColors` on new positions)
- Remove all edgeMesh references from `draw()`, `setOpacity()`, `cleanup()`

Step 4: Update vertex shader `src/visual/shaders/terrainVertex.vert.glsl`
- Add continuous time-based wave animation that runs independently of audio:
  `baseWave = fbm3(pos.xz * 0.15, time * 0.0003 * cadence) * heightScale * motionAmplitude`
  This ensures waves visibly roll across the surface even with no audio
- Bass SCALES wave amplitude: multiply baseWave by `(1.0 + bassEnergy * 1.5)` instead of adding a separate bass displacement
- Treble shimmer: keep existing jitter logic but add per-point size variation
- Adjust point size formula for small dense points: base size ~1.5-3px
  `pointSize = 2.5 * (1200.0 / depth) * trebleSparkle * atmosphericDecay`
  `gl_PointSize = clamp(pointSize, 1.0, 6.0)` — much smaller range than current 2-40
- Keep pointer disturbance, fog factor, vertex color passing

Step 5: Update fragment shader `src/visual/shaders/terrainVertex.frag.glsl`
- Soft round sprite with alpha falloff: `pointAlpha = 1.0 - smoothstep(0.0, 0.5, dist)` (smoother center glow)
- Keep additive blending (already set on material)
- Keep existing palette/fog/bass-alpha logic — it already works well
- Optionally boost base lightness slightly for glowing dot appearance

Step 6: Update camera framing for low-angle horizon view
- In `src/visual/systems/terrainHeightfield.ts`, adjust terrain group position and rotation:
  - Position: `(0, -1.5, -2)` — closer and less below camera
  - Rotation: `(-Math.PI * 0.38, 0, 0)` — steeper tilt to skim surface toward horizon
- In `src/visual/cameraMotion.ts`, adjust BASE_Y slightly lower (~-0.3) and BASE_Z slightly closer (~4) so the camera skims the wave surface
- Keep autonomous harmonic drift but constrain Y amplitude to avoid flying too far above/below the terrain plane

Step 7: Update `src/main.ts` wiring
- Pass `pointCount: quality.terrainPointCount` to `createTerrainHeightfield()` at line 161
- The system registry factory at line 191 already passes config through, so it will pick up the new field automatically

Step 8: Clean up removed code
- Delete edge shaders `src/visual/shaders/terrain.vert.glsl` and `src/visual/shaders/terrain.frag.glsl` (no longer used)
- Delete `src/visual/generators/terrainHeightfield.ts` (replaced by terrainParticleSheet)
- Remove `extractVertices.ts` import from system if it becomes unused (check other consumers first)
- Remove `terrainRows`/`terrainCols` from quality profile IF no other system uses them; otherwise keep for backward compat with compound modes

Step 9: Verify shader integrity and build
- Run `npm run build` to confirm no compile errors
- Open dev server and check browser console for WebGL/shader validation errors
- Verify: no LineSegments in scene, only Points
- Verify: point count matches tier expectations
- Verify: waves animate continuously without audio
- Verify: bass modulates wave height, treble modulates point shimmer/size
- Verify: no visible grid pattern (jitter is working)
- Verify: camera angle skims surface toward horizon
testing_recommendations: Build gate: `npm run build` must pass with zero errors
Shader integrity: Open browser dev tools console — zero WebGL compile/validate errors
Visual check: No LineSegments visible in scene (use Three.js inspector or scene.traverse to confirm only Points objects)
Point count verification: Log geometry.attributes.position.count per tier — should be ~20k/60k/120k
Jitter verification: Zoom into particle sheet — points should NOT form visible rows or columns
Continuous animation: Mute audio and observe — waves must still roll across surface over time
Bass reactivity: Play bass-heavy track — wave amplitude should visibly increase
Treble reactivity: Play treble-heavy track — points should shimmer/sparkle in size
Camera framing: Screenshot should show low-angle view skimming wave surface toward horizon
Reduced motion: Set `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` in browser — wave amplitude should decrease but not stop
Quality tier fallback: Force each tier (low/medium/high) and verify scene renders correctly with appropriate point density
Performance: Check frame rate stays above 30fps on medium tier (~60k points)
No storage APIs: Search console/network for localStorage, sessionStorage, cookie access — must be zero
risks: Shader compilation errors from modified GLSL — mitigate by making incremental changes and testing after each shader edit
Performance regression at 120k points on high tier — mitigate by keeping point size small and using simple fragment shader (soft disc only, no expensive per-fragment noise)
Removing edge shaders may break compound modes that reference the terrain system — check `compoundModes.ts` for terrain references before deleting
Camera angle change may cause terrain to clip out of view or look wrong with other visual modes in rotation — test terrain mode in isolation AND in mode rotation
The existing `extractUniqueVertices` utility may be used by other systems — grep before removing
Jitter algorithm must be deterministic (seeded PRNG) to maintain session-reproducibility guarantee
